english: frq. with snaṅ (snang): raṅ-snaṅ (rang snang) ˳krul-par ('khrul par) ˳dug ('dug) I have been mistaken, it was a deception of the senses Mil.; snaṅ-˳krul (snang 'khrul), and ˳krul-snaṅ ('khrul snang) illusion, delusion; ˳krul-snaṅ-ćan ('khrul snang can) delusive Glr.; to err, as a syn. of nór-ba (nor ba): kyód-ćag (khyod cag) ˳krul-pai ('khrul pa'i) jíg-rten-pa ('jig rten pa) ye deluded children of the world! Mil.; źes (zhes) ˳dón-pa-rnams ('don pa rnams) ˳krul ('khrul) they who pronounce (read) in this manner, are mistaken; ˳a ('a) ˳dogs ('dogs) ˳krul ('khrul) the adding of ˳a ('a) is a mistake; nor-˳krul (nor 'khrul) mistake, nor-˳krul (nor 'khrul) sél-ba (sel ba) Schr., *tón-će, sál-po gyáb-će* (ton ce, sal po rgyab ce), W. to remove mistakes, to correct. — 3. to be insane, deranged, syn. of smyós-pa (smyos pa) Dzl. and others. — ˳krul-pa ('khrul pa) 1. adj. mistaken, deceived. — 2. sbst. mistake; frenzy, madness: ˳krul-gźi ('khrul gzhi) mistake, error; ˳krul-so ('khrul so) (errandi locus) occasion for committing mistakes, a wrong way, peril; mistake, error, cf. gól-sa (gol sa); ˳krul-˳kór ('khrul 'khor) artifice Sch., (Cs.: machine, contrivance: but this is spelled more correctly ˳prul-˳kór ('phrul 'khor)).
1. to wish, to long for, zas-skóm (zas skom) Med., kyím-la (khyim la) Lex. — 2. W. to look upon with envy, jealousy.
pf. krós (khros), to be angry, la (la) at.

to roar, rush, buzz, hum, rná-ba (rna ba) kúr-la (khur la) ˳krog ('khrog) Med., a tingling noise is caused in the ear; rgyu-lóṅ (rgyu long) ˳króg-ćiṅ ('khrog cing) a rumbling in the bowels Med.; sbo-˳króg (sbo 'khrog) in the belly; ˳krog-˳króg ('khrog 'khrog) roaring, rushing, buzzing.

pf. and fut. dkról (dkrol), imp. król (khrol) 1. to cause to sound, to make a noise, to play, ról-mo (rol mo) on an instrument, to ring (a bell), to beat (a gong, cymbal): ma (ma) dkról-bar (dkrol bar) without being played on. — 2. to sound, resound, *dód-pa ṭól-la rag* (dod pa tol la rag) W. my bowels croak: ˳król-po ('khrol po) a player, performer, bell-ringer etc., cf. król-po (khrol po): *trol-lo-lo-tsé* (trol lo lo tse) W. a tinkling of bells.

1. the letter g (ga), originally, and in the border countries still at the present time, as initial letter = the English hard g, as final letter = ck; in C. as initial deep-toned and aspirated (gh), as final letter more or less indistinct; as a prefix (in Khams and Balti) fricative = γ or χ: v. Preface. — 2. as numerical figure: 3, cf. ka (ka) 2.
affix (article) to some substantives, like ka (ka).
C. (pron. *gha-ṭal* (gha tal)) tax, duty (on cattle and butter).

W. a title of honour: the old gentleman, the old squire e.g. *ga-gá ta-ra-ćán* (ga ga ta ra can) the old Squire Tara Chand, opp. to no-nó (no no) the young Squire; instead of it in C.: *a-jho-lág* (a jho lag).
tickling Cs.; ga-ga-tsíl (ga ga tshil) byéd-pa (byed pa) to tickle.
such a one, such a thing Cs.; such and such; v. će-ge-mo (ce ge mo).
a melon Cs. (some Lexx. have: cucumber, others: barley).
some (people), a good many; a good deal W., C.
without cause, involuntarily, e.g. to weep Med.
Ssk., ga-taí (ga ta'i) sde-tsan (sde tshan) a particular kind of Indian hand-writing, besides Nagari and Lantsa Glr.
